name: Cabo de Santas Cruzes - Round trip along the southern coast of Tarragona
name_original: Cabo de Santas Cruzes - Ida y vuelta por el litoral meridional de Tarragona
region_intro: Hiking around L'Ametlla De Mar offers diverse coastal landscapes along Catalonia's Costa Daurada. The region is characterized by its numerous pristine coves, dramatic cliffs, and shaded pine forests that often extend to the sea. Trails frequently follow the Mediterranean coastline, providing varied terrain with constant ascents and descents. This area combines natural beauty with historical elements, making it suitable for a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around L'Ametlla De Mar The most popular hiking route is l'Ametlla de Mar to l'Ampolla, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route follows a scenic stretch of the GR 92 Coastal Path, offering views of the Mediterranean. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabo de Santas Cruzes - Round trip along the southern coast of Tarragona, a moderate 11.7 miles (18.8 km) path. This trail explores the rugged coastline, passing by several coves and natural features. Local hikers also love the Cala Llenya Beach – Cala de Santes Creus loop from Camping Ametlla, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through coastal vegetation and past notable coves, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around L'Ametlla De Mar is defined by coastal paths, diverse coves, and pine forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and more challenging routes. The routes in L'Ametlla De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore L'Ametlla De Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The hiking trails in L'Ametlla de Mar primarily feature diverse coastal landscapes. You'll encounter rugged paths with constant ascents and descents, often running close to the sea. Expect a mix of fine sandy stretches, isolated rocky coves, and shaded pine forests that extend right to the water's edge. The GR 92 Coastal Path, for instance, is known for its impressive views and varied coastal terrain.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: Yes, L'Ametlla de Mar offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities. There are over 60 routes available, catering to various preferences. These include 43 easy trails and 23 moderate trails, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical points of interest. You can explore numerous pristine coves like &lt;strong&gt;Cala de les Santes Creus&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;Cala de l'Estany Podrit&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;Cala Forn Beach&lt;/strong&gt;. Don't miss &lt;strong&gt;L'Illot Rock and Beach&lt;/strong&gt;, a unique small island almost connected to the mainland. Another intriguing spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llop Marí Cove and Cave&lt;/a&gt;, steeped in local legends. Hikers will also find remnants of ancient watchtowers and fortifications along the coast.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The trails in L'Ametlla de Mar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning coastal scenery, the opportunity to discover diverse coves, and the picturesque pine forests that line the routes. The combination of natural beauty and varied terrain makes for a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, L'Ametlla de Mar offers several circular routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14890176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Llenya Beach – Cala de Santes Creus loop from Camping Ametlla&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10 km trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951752173/cabo-de-santas-cruzes-round-trip-along-the-southern-coast-of-tarragona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo de Santas Cruzes - Round trip along the southern coast of Tarragona&lt;/a&gt;, covering 18.8 km.

Q: Can I find easy hiking trails suitable for families in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: Yes, L'Ametlla de Mar has 43 easy trails, many of which are suitable for families. These routes often follow the coastline, offering gentle walks with beautiful views and opportunities to stop at beaches and coves. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly tagged, the abundance of easy options means you can find suitable paths for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The trails in L'Ametlla de Mar can be enjoyed year-round. The Mediterranean climate means mild winters, making it pleasant for hiking. During warmer months, the numerous coves and beaches along the routes offer opportunities for refreshing dips in crystal-clear waters, which can be a welcome break during your hike.

Q: Are there any specific beaches or coves that are highlights along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The coastline of L'Ametlla de Mar features over thirty unique beaches and coves. Highlights include &lt;strong&gt;Cala de les Santes Creus&lt;/strong&gt;, a pristine pebble beach along the GR 92, and &lt;strong&gt;Cala de l'Estany Podrit&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its pine forest extending to the sea. &lt;strong&gt;Playa de l'Illot&lt;/strong&gt;, with its small island, is another magnificent spot. Many of these offer transparent waters perfect for swimming or snorkeling breaks.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points, especially near beaches or villages, will have designated parking areas. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for information on parking facilities near the starting point. Public transport options are generally limited for reaching more remote trailheads.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: While there isn't a universal rule, generally, dogs are welcome on many of the natural trails and coastal paths in L'Ametlla de Mar, especially if kept on a leash. However, some protected areas or specific beaches might have restrictions, particularly during peak season. Always be mindful of local signage and ensure your dog does not disturb wildlife or other hikers.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the hikes?
A: The hikes in L'Ametlla de Mar offer breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea. You'll often find yourself on impressive cliffs overlooking coves, or walking through pine forests with glimpses of the deep blue waters. The section of the GR 92 Coastal Path is particularly renowned for its stunning coastal vistas.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails that pass through L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: Yes, the iconic GR 92 Coastal Path, also known as the Mediterranean Trail, passes through L'Ametlla de Mar. The section connecting L'Ametlla de Mar to L'Ampolla is particularly famous for being one of the most beautiful stretches of the Catalan coastline. You can hike a portion of this trail, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1734214519/l-ametlla-de-mar-to-l-ampolla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;l'Ametlla de Mar to l'Ampolla&lt;/a&gt; route.
tips:
Estany Beach is located to the south of the Estany Natural Port and is included in the Cap de Santes Creus Area of Natural Interest.
It is approximately 30 m wide. and a length of 200 m. it has a bar, accessibility by car, has daily cleaning, red cross, accessibility on foot, is sandy, has a shower and toilet.
Relatively difficult access beach located between L'Ametlla de Mar and L'Ampolla. The beach also has the presence of a small lake.
On Illot beach, there is a small island that is almost attached to the beach. It is a beautiful place, with a rugged and wild environment.
Rocky cove like others in the area, such as Llosar de Caballé and Garretes. All these beaches and coves are characterized by their tranquility, their natural beauty and their crystal clear waters.

The cove of Estany Podrit is a shelter of fine sand and rocks, located on the path of the GR 92 route integrated into the ring road. It is easily reached by an unpaved road and, although it does not have a car park as such, it is possible to get there by car if you leave it at the end of the road.
Sheltered by its pine and scrub area, the cove stretches for 60 meters in length and is 15 meters wide. It has a low occupancy rate, which, together with its natural environment, gives it an attractive virgin appearance. Moderate waves can be easily reached without a steep slope. It does not have services, except showers.

The waters of this cove are attractive to fishermen. The most common species is the crab, both the so-called hermit and the green, which can be easily caught with a hook. The Ametlla de Mar marina is also nearby for sailing enthusiasts.
